rawIntent:
[Feature: Add a single GET /uptime route that returns JSON {uptimeSeconds: floor of proces — Phase 3: Phase 3: GET /uptime route handler and barrel export]

Create src/modules/uptime/uptime.route.ts with an Express Router that handles GET / and returns JSON from UptimeService.getUptime(). Create src/modules/uptime/index.ts as a barrel export re-exporting UptimeStatus, IUptimeService, UptimeService, and the router. This phase depends on src/modules/uptime/uptime.model.ts, src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.interface.ts, and src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.ts from Phases 1-2 — read all three before generating.

This phase depends on: Phase 1, Phase 2.

Phase architecture notes:
Route handler follows Express Router pattern. Barrel export mirrors src/modules/status/index.ts.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):
{"interfaces":["File: src/modules/uptime/uptime.routes.ts\nimport { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';\nimport { UptimeService } from './uptime.service';\n\nexport async function uptimeRoutes(fastify: FastifyInstance): Promise<void> {\n  fastify.get('/uptime', async (request, reply) => {\n    try {\n      const status = UptimeService.getUptime();\n      return reply.status(200).send(status);\n    } catch (error) {\n      request.log.error(error);\n      return reply.status(500).send({ error: 'Internal Server Error' });\n    }\n  });\n}","File: src/modules/uptime/index.ts\nexport { UptimeStatus } from './uptime.model';\nexport { IUptimeService } from './uptime.service.interface';\nexport { UptimeService } from './uptime.service';\nexport { uptimeRoutes } from './uptime.routes';"],"importStatements":["import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';","import { UptimeService } from './uptime.service';","export { UptimeStatus } from './uptime.model';","export { IUptimeService } from './uptime.service.interface';","export { UptimeService } from './uptime.service';","export { uptimeRoutes } from './uptime.routes';"],"successCriteria":["src/modules/uptime/uptime.routes.ts exists and exports an async function uptimeRoutes(fastify: FastifyInstance): Promise<void> that registers GET /uptime","src/modules/uptime/index.ts exists and re-exports UptimeStatus, IUptimeService, UptimeService, and uptimeRoutes","GET /uptime returns HTTP 200 with JSON body { uptimeSeconds: number } where number equals Math.floor(process.uptime())","The route handler does not produce unhandled promise rejections (GP-006)","No database access, no audit records, no RBAC, no input validation required (GP-001, GP-002, GP-003, GP-005 not applicable)"]}

successCriteria.description: src/modules/uptime/uptime.routes.ts exists and exports an async function uptimeRoutes(fastify: FastifyInstance): Promise<void> that registers GET /uptime
src/modules/uptime/index.ts exists and re-exports UptimeStatus, IUptimeService, UptimeService, and uptimeRoutes
GET /uptime returns HTTP 200 with JSON body { uptimeSeconds: number } where number equals Math.floor(process.uptime())
The route handler catches errors and does not produce unhandled promise rejections (GP-006)

constraints: GP-006: No unhandled promise rejections — the route handler must wrap its logic in try/catch and return a proper error response
Use FastifyInstance (from Fastify, the declared framework in HARNESS.json) — not Express
No database access, no audit records, no RBAC, no input validation required for this route (GP-001, GP-002, GP-003, GP-005 not applicable)
outOfScope: Phase 4: Registering the uptime route in src/app.ts
Any database access or repository pattern usage
Input validation (no request body or query params to validate)
